plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs to reduce risk.
Significantly trim concentrated positions like SPY and SMH.
Increase allocation to the income-focused QQQI.
Add a new, high-quality healthcare name (XLV) from the scanner.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a larger cash buffer.
Expect a lower target return and reduced drawdown potential.
conservative.read: This plan aims for capital preservation with a target monthly return around 1.5%. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, holding a substantial cash buffer. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs like SOXL, reducing overall exposure.
Reduce concentration in SPY and SMH to meet mandate limits.
Increase position in the strong performer CRWD.
Add a new, high-quality tech name (MSFT) from the scanner.
This plan balances growth with risk reduction, maintaining some leverage.
Expect a moderate target return around 2.5% per month.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly return by managing risk first. It reduces concentration and leverage while adding to strong performers and quality setups. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged ETF SOXL for amplified gains.
Trim broad index positions SPY and QQQ to reduce overall exposure.
Further increase position in the strong performer CRWD.
Add new, high-scoring tech names (DDOG, NET) from the scanner.
This plan aggressively pursues growth by concentrating in top setups and leverage.
Expect a higher target return around 4% per month, with significantly higher potential drawdowns.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% monthly return by leaning into leverage and high-conviction setups. It accepts higher concentration and increased drawdown risk, including leveraged ETF decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Eliminate all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Sell volatile sector holdings like SMH.
Maximize allocation to income-focused QQQI for distributions.
Increase allocation to the broad index SPY for stability.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly income and capital preservation.
Expect a low target return around 1% per month with minimal drawdowns.
income.read: This income-focused plan aims for a target return of about 1% per month, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdowns. It shifts heavily into income-generating ETFs like QQQI and broad index ETFs like SPY, minimizing volatile assets. NAV can still fall in market downturns.
